new.explanation: We are given the inequation \(2x + 8 > 14\) and need to isolate x. Since every operation we use (subtracting a constant, then dividing by a positive coefficient) preserves the direction of an inequality, the inequality sign will never need to be reversed.

Subtract 8 from both sides to remove the constant term from the left-hand side, leaving the term containing x isolated on the left. Subtracting this term from both sides removes it from that side so the variable term can be isolated.
Divide both sides by 2, the coefficient of x. Because 2 is positive, the direction of the inequality symbol does not change.
The solution set is every real number strictly greater than 3. Any value of x larger than 3 will make the original inequation true.
We are given the inequation \(x - 5 < 2\) and need to isolate x. Only an addition step is required, so the inequality direction is preserved throughout.
Add 5 to both sides to cancel the -5 on the left-hand side. Adding this term to both sides moves it across the equals or inequality sign so the variable terms can be collected together.
Simplify the right-hand side by combining the constants. Keeping the expression in its simplest form makes the next step easier to carry out accurately.

The solution is all real numbers strictly less than 7, meaning any x-value below 7 satisfies the original inequation. This step works toward isolating the value we are solving for, building on the previous line of working.

We are given \(-1 - x \ge 6\) and need to isolate x. Because we will need to multiply or divide by a negative number to make the coefficient of x positive, the inequality sign must be reversed at that step.
Add 1 to both sides to remove the constant from the left-hand side. Adding this term to both sides moves it across the equals or inequality sign so the variable terms can be collected together.
Multiply both sides by -1 to make the coefficient of x positive. Multiplying an inequality by a negative number reverses its direction, so \(\ge\) becomes \(\le\).

The solution is all real numbers less than or equal to -7, meaning x can be -7 or any smaller value. This step works toward isolating the value we are solving for, building on the previous line of working.
We are given \(4x \ge -12\) and need to isolate x by dividing by the coefficient of x. Since 4 is positive, dividing both sides by it will not change the direction of the inequality.
Divide both sides by 4. Dividing removes the coefficient from the variable term so that the variable is left on its own.
Simplify the fraction on the right-hand side. Keeping the expression in its simplest form makes the next step easier to carry out accurately.

The solution is all real numbers greater than or equal to -3, meaning x can equal -3 or take any larger value. This step works toward isolating the value we are solving for, building on the previous line of working.

We are given \(2 - 4x \le 14 + x\) and want to collect the x terms on one side and the constants on the other. We will finally divide by a positive coefficient of x, so the inequality direction is preserved throughout.

Add 4x to both sides so that the x term only appears on the right-hand side. Adding this term to both sides moves it across the equals or inequality sign so the variable terms can be collected together.
Subtract 14 from both sides to isolate the term containing x. Subtracting this term from both sides removes it from that side so the variable term can be isolated.
Divide both sides by 5, which is positive, so the inequality direction stays the same. Rewriting with x on the left gives the final answer, meaning x can equal -2.4 or any larger value.

Let n be the number of lights hired. The total cost is the fixed call-out fee of 250 dollars plus 60 dollars for each light, and this total must not exceed the 800 dollar budget. We set up an inequation and solve for n.
Subtract 250 from both sides to isolate the term containing n. Subtracting this term from both sides removes it from that side so the variable term can be isolated.
Divide both sides by 60, which is positive, so the inequality direction is unchanged. Dividing removes the coefficient from the variable term so that the variable is left on its own.
Since n must be a whole number of lights and n can be at most 9.1666..., the largest possible whole number of lights that stays within budget is 9.
We are given \(3x - 1 \le -10\) and need to isolate x. We will only add a constant and divide by a positive coefficient, so the inequality direction is preserved.
Add 1 to both sides to remove the constant term from the left-hand side. Adding this term to both sides moves it across the equals or inequality sign so the variable terms can be collected together.
Divide both sides by 3, which is positive, so the direction of the inequality is unchanged. Dividing removes the coefficient from the variable term so that the variable is left on its own.

The solution is all real numbers less than or equal to -3, meaning x can equal -3 or any smaller value. This step works toward isolating the value we are solving for, building on the previous line of working.
We are given \(10 - 2x < 4\) and need to isolate x. Because the coefficient of x is negative, dividing by it at the final step will require reversing the inequality direction.
Subtract 10 from both sides to remove the constant from the left-hand side. Subtracting this term from both sides removes it from that side so the variable term can be isolated.
Divide both sides by -2. Dividing an inequality by a negative number reverses its direction, so \(<\) becomes \(>\).

The solution is all real numbers strictly greater than 3, meaning any x-value above 3 satisfies the original inequation. This step works toward isolating the value we are solving for, building on the previous line of working.

Let x be the number of votes for Jack and y be the number of votes for Jill. All 31 members voted, so \(x + y = 31\), and we are told Jack received more than twice as many votes as Jill, so \(x > 2y\). We substitute to get an inequation in x alone.
Expand the right-hand side and collect the x terms on one side. Expanding the bracket converts it into separate terms that can then be combined or compared.
Divide both sides by 3, which is positive, so the inequality direction is unchanged. Dividing removes the coefficient from the variable term so that the variable is left on its own.
Since x must be a whole number of votes and x must be strictly greater than 20.67, the smallest whole number satisfying this is 21. So Jack needed at least 21 votes.
We are given \(4 - 3x > 13\) and need to isolate x. Because the coefficient of x is negative, dividing by it at the final step will require reversing the inequality direction.
Subtract 4 from both sides to remove the constant from the left-hand side. Subtracting this term from both sides removes it from that side so the variable term can be isolated.
Divide both sides by -3. Dividing by a negative number reverses the inequality, so \(>\) becomes \(<\).

The solution is all real numbers strictly less than -3, meaning any x-value below -3 satisfies the original inequation. This step works toward isolating the value we are solving for, building on the previous line of working.

We are given \(15 - 8x > -3x - 10\) and want to collect the x terms on one side and the constants on the other. We will end by dividing by a positive coefficient of x, so the inequality direction is preserved throughout.
Add 8x to both sides so the x term only appears on the right-hand side. Adding this term to both sides moves it across the equals or inequality sign so the variable terms can be collected together.
Add 10 to both sides to isolate the term containing x. Adding this term to both sides moves it across the equals or inequality sign so the variable terms can be collected together.
Divide both sides by 5, which is positive, so the direction is unchanged. Rewriting with x on the left gives the final answer, meaning x can be any value below 5.

We are given \(-x \le 5\) and need the coefficient of x to be positive, so we will multiply both sides by -1, which reverses the inequality direction. Multiplying clears the coefficient or fraction so that the variable term is left in a simpler form.
Multiply both sides by -1. Since we are multiplying by a negative number, the inequality symbol reverses from \(\le\) to \(\ge\).
There is no further simplification needed; the coefficient of x is now 1. This step works toward isolating the value we are solving for, building on the previous line of working.

The solution is all real numbers greater than or equal to -5, meaning x can equal -5 or take any larger value. This step works toward isolating the value we are solving for, building on the previous line of working.

We are given \(5x + 2 \ge 2x + 11\) and want to collect the x terms on one side and the constants on the other. We will finish by dividing by a positive coefficient of x, so the inequality direction is preserved.
Subtract 2x from both sides so the x terms only appear on the left-hand side. Subtracting this term from both sides removes it from that side so the variable term can be isolated.
Subtract 2 from both sides to isolate the term containing x. Subtracting this term from both sides removes it from that side so the variable term can be isolated.
Divide both sides by 3, which is positive, so the direction is unchanged. The solution means x can equal 3 or any larger value.

Add 4 to both sides to isolate the term containing x. Adding this term to both sides moves it across the equals or inequality sign so the variable terms can be collected together.

\(4x - 5\) being 'below' the line \(10 - x\) means \(4x - 5 < 10 - x\). We set up this inequation and solve for x, collecting x terms on one side and constants on the other.
Add x to both sides so the x terms only appear on the left-hand side. Adding this term to both sides moves it across the equals or inequality sign so the variable terms can be collected together.
Add 5 to both sides to isolate the term containing x. Adding this term to both sides moves it across the equals or inequality sign so the variable terms can be collected together.
Divide both sides by 5, which is positive, so the direction is unchanged. The solution means x can be any value below 3.

Add 10 to both sides to isolate the term containing x. Adding this term to both sides moves it across the equals or inequality sign so the variable terms can be collected together.
